_doc: cnos#568 (CDS issue-state FSM, Phase 1) — the single declarative source
of truth for CDS issue-status transitions (AC1). This is package-owned
data (cnos.cds), consumed by the generic, package-agnostic evaluator
engine never hardcodes a CDS state name in a switch/if-chain: it looks
up the current state's entry in `transitions` below and evaluates each
`rules[]` guard against the observed FactSnapshot in order, first match
wins. Guard names reference the fixed, generic predicate vocabulary
implemented once in table.go's guard registry (run_active,
branch_has_commits, pr_exists, ... — see `guards` below); adding a new
CDS state or rule requires editing only this file, not Go source.
Phase 1 is read-only: every `action` here is a *proposal* the CLI
prints, never an executed mutation (no label writes anywhere in this

guards.run_active: the issue's dispatch workflow run is queued or in_progress
guards.branch_has_commits: cycle/{issue} has commits beyond its base (non-empty diff)
guards.pr_exists: a pull request for the issue exists
guards.pr_has_commits: the PR has commits beyond its base
guards.repair_contract_present: a repair contract / prior beta or delta findings are present (e.g. delta-repair.md, or beta-review.md with an iterate verdict) — required for changes -> todo repair re-entry per cnos#516
guards.cdd_artifacts_present: at least one CDD artifact exists under .cdd/unreleased/{issue}/
guards.checks_passing: the most recently observed CI/checks state is success

_doc_phase2: cnos#569 (Phase 2, authority flip) adds the first two rules under
state "in-progress" below: a worker (concretely: the δ wake-invoked
cycle, cds-dispatch/SKILL.md step 7) that has produced
REVIEW-REQUEST.yml and deliverable matter (a PR and/or commits beyond
base) REQUESTS the in-progress -> review transition by running
`cn issues fsm evaluate --issue {N} --apply`; the FSM proposes (and,
with --apply, applies) the transition only when review_request_present
is true AND at least one of pr_exists / pr_has_commits /
branch_has_commits is true. Deliberately NOT gated on all_false:
[run_active] (unlike the pre-existing dead-in-progress rules further
down): the primary caller is the worker's OWN wake requesting the
transition synchronously, at a point where its own workflow run is
still "in_progress" by definition -- gating on a dead run would make
AC2's entire request-your-own-transition mechanism unusable in its
main use case. A second new rule blocks (outcome: blocked) the
symmetric case -- review_request_present true but no deliverable
matter -- so a worker that writes REVIEW-REQUEST.yml without doing
real work is refused, not silently repurposed into some other
transition (AC3: empty-review is structurally impossible). Neither
rule changes any existing in-progress fixture's outcome: both require
review_request_present == true, which no Phase-1 fixture sets while
in-progress (see issues-fsm/issuesfsm_test.go's
TestSeam_CellKindNotEnforced-adjacent backward-compat coverage).

rules.reason: status:ready has no Phase-1 gating guard; release for dispatch (operator flips ready -> todo).
status:todo is already dispatchable; no evaluator action needed.

cnos#569 Phase 2 / cnos#574 AC3: review requested (REVIEW-REQUEST.yml present) with a PR and commits beyond base on that PR; in-progress -> review requested by the worker. Branch-commits-only (no PR) no longer qualifies (cnos#574 tightened this from any_true to all_true, aligning the FSM with the closeout-integrity preflight's PR-required rule).

cnos#574 AC3: review requested (REVIEW-REQUEST.yml present) but missing a PR and/or commits beyond base on that PR (branch-commits-only no longer qualifies) -- refusing to propose in-progress -> review. This rule is unconditional given review_request_present==true and is positioned after rule 1, so it fires on ANY partial-evidence combination rule 1 didn't match (not just all-three-false) -- see cnos#574 self-coherence.md Friction-notes writeup.
workflow run is active (queued/in_progress); awaiting completion, no transition proposed.

dead run with matter: no active run, but cycle/{issue} has commits beyond base and/or a PR exists. Never re-dispatch blind over existing work (cnos#368 failure mode) -- propose delta-recovery (open/request a recovery PR) instead.
dead run with no matter: no active run, branch absent/empty, no PR -- safe to requeue to status:todo.

cnos#574 AC2: review evidence present (REVIEW-REQUEST.yml AND an open PR AND commits beyond base on that PR); status:review is valid. review_request_present alone (or any two of the three) is no longer sufficient -- cnos#574 tightened this from any_true to all_true.

cnos#574 AC2: status:review requires REVIEW-REQUEST.yml AND an open PR AND commits beyond base on that PR; at least one is missing -- refusing to validate an under-evidenced review. This rule is unconditional and positioned after rule 1, so it fires on ANY partial-evidence combination (not just all-three-false) -- see cnos#574 self-coherence.md Friction-notes writeup.
repair contract / prior beta-delta findings present; changes -> todo repair re-entry enabled (repair_pass).
changes -> todo blocked: no repair contract or prior beta/delta findings present; repair re-entry requires repair context (cnos#516).
